content: One of the changes that the Rust helpers inlining patch makes is that helpers get linked into both the core kernel and any kernel modules containing Rust. I wonder if it makes sense to also do this when not performing helpers LTO so that unused helpers can be dead-code eliminated by the linker. (Since the sections containing the helpers are not referenced by anything.)
Relocatable linking w/ GC sections require explicit specificification of roots and I doubt that the kernel is doing it. Even gc-sections on vmlinux is considered experimental.
fair enough, sounds like it won't help
